title: ROV Operations and Subsea Inspection
entry_mechanism: Saudi ROV company established through Aramco supply chain development with international ROV operator JV
executive_summary:
Remotely Operated Vehicles (ROVs) are underwater robots used for subsea pipeline inspection, platform structural inspection, seabed survey, and emergency intervention in offshore oil and gas operations. Saudi Aramco operates one of the world's most extensive offshore infrastructure networks — Safaniya, Manifa, Zuluf — requiring continuous subsea inspection. Today, all ROV operations are performed by foreign specialist companies (Oceaneering, Subsea 7, TechnipFMC) under Aramco contracts.

Node MR-6 targets the establishment of a Saudi ROV operations company — initially operating foreign-manufactured ROVs under a technical partnership, progressing to Saudi ROV payload integration and eventually Saudi ROV component manufacturing. The operations capability is Phase II because it requires the maritime technician workforce and quality management systems established in Phase I.

The global ROV services market was valued at $6.2 billion in 2024. Saudi Aramco's annual ROV services spend is estimated at $150–250 million. A Saudi ROV operations company capturing 50% of Aramco's work within Phase II would represent $75–125 million in annual Saudi-retained revenue.

companies.name: Oceaneering International
companies.rationale: Oceaneering is the world's largest ROV operator with the largest Saudi Aramco contract. A Saudi JV with Oceaneering transfers operational management to Saudi company.
companies.contact: Oceaneering VP International Operations, Middle East

companies.name: Subsea 7
companies.rationale: Subsea 7 provides ROV and subsea services to Aramco. Norwegian/UK partnership for Saudi ROV company establishment.

companies.name: Forum Energy Technologies
companies.rationale: Forum manufactures ROV systems and has technology licensing programmes. Saudi ROV company using Forum ROV equipment.

companies.name: Saab Seaeye
companies.rationale: Saab Seaeye produces electric ROVs and has an international sales and partnership network. Non-ITAR technology.

companies.name: Fugro
companies.rationale: Fugro provides ROV survey and inspection services and has established local partnerships in multiple markets.
companies.contact: Fugro VP Business Development, Middle East

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Offshore Engineering
partners.current: ROV services fully outsourced to foreign operators
partners.upgrade: Aramco Saudi ROV supply chain development programme; 10-year commitment to Saudi ROV company for incremental work share; Aramco technical supervision and quality oversight
partners.timeline: 24 months to first Saudi ROV operation

partners.name: SAMI Maritime Division
partners.current: Naval programme management; some marine services
partners.upgrade: SAMI-Oceaneering ROV operations JV; Saudi ROV pilot training programme; fleet of 5 ROVs initially; expand to 20 ROVs by Phase II end

partners.name: Saudi Aramco Research Centre
partners.current: Subsea materials research; inspection technology research
partners.upgrade: Saudi ROV AI-assisted inspection research; Saudi-developed pipeline inspection algorithm; autonomous ROV mission planning research

partners.name: TVTC Maritime Training
partners.upgrade: ROV pilot and technician training programme; IMCA ROV certification pathway; 30 Saudi ROV pilots per year
